reasoning: The question asks whether the number of ACLED 'Battles' in specific 30-day windows in TCI will exceed the 360-day moving average. The baseline 360-day average ending late May 2025 is 0.000. Under ACLED coding definitions, TCI gang violence (despite a record 48 homicides in 2024) is predominantly coded as 'Violence Against Civilians' rather than 'Battles', which require two-sided combat between organized armed groups. Extensive searches for events in May and early June 2025 found no police shootouts or gang-on-gang skirmishes that would be coded as Battles. Thus, the expected rate of Battles in any 30-day window is firmly anchored near zero. I am estimating the likelihood of >=1 Battle happening in these future 30-day periods at roughly the minimum acceptable lower bound (5%), gradually scaled up to 7% for the remote horizon to reflect general uncertainty.
